metadata.title: Understanding Matano Fault, Southeast Arm of Sulawesi, Indonesia
metadata.journal: Proceedings GEOSEA XIV Congress & 45th IAGI Annual Convention
metadata.study_area: Northern Southeast Arm of Sulawesi; Matano Fault zone and Lake Matano pull-apart basin
metadata.method: DEM analysis (IFSAR-derived), geological map interpretation (Simandjuntak et al. 1993; Effendi & Bawono 1997), tectonic stress inversion from 9 earthquake focal mechanisms (Win-Tensor 5.0.7)
conclusions: Matano Fault is a left-stepping, left-lateral strike-slip fault with ESE-WNW orientation, connecting Palu-Koro Fault (west) to Sorong Fault (east)
The left-stepping geometry creates a transtension zone forming Matano Lake as a pull-apart basin
Minimum displacement: ~60 km left-lateral, inferred from offset of Tokala Formation on the eastern coast of the Southeast Arm
Tectonic stress inversion of 9 focal mechanisms (M>4.5 since 1970) gives maximum compressive stress at N38°E/30° (NE-SW sigma1)
Fault activated/reactivated after Early Miocene collision event; located within the Sulawesi Ophiolite Belt

properties.name: Matano Fault
properties.displacement_marker: Tokala Formation offset on eastern SE Arm coast
properties.age_activity: Early Miocene reactivation, seismically active to present
properties.sigma1_description: N38°E/30° from focal mechanism stress inversion (9 events M>4.5, 1970-2016)
properties.context: Located within Sulawesi Ophiolite Belt; connects PKF (west) to Sorong Fault (east); parallel to Lawanopo and Kolaka faults
